meta.title: Brain-Score · Unified Model Interface
meta.subtitle: Register a model once. Score it across vision, language, audio, video, multimodal, perturbation, and embodied benchmarks — through one process() interface.
meta.note: Numbers are raw scores from the v1.5 interface. Every capability is reported against a matched null floor; a score that doesn't clear its null is reporting noise, and we say so.
meta.provenance: Neural/behavioral scores reproduced bit-for-bit on v1.5 from the v1 baselines; game scores from scripts/vlm_game; figures from brainscore.visualization.

language_encoding.reading: Pretraining contributes ~3.8x over the random-feature floor; bigger causal LMs (Qwen, BLIP-2 OPT) are far more language-aligned than CLIP.

it_encoding.reading: All models clear the random floor (3.6x). CLIP ViT-B/32 leads despite being smallest — contrastive image-text training beats scale for IT alignment.

video_encoding.capability: Neural encoding — video, visual ROI (Lahner2024, r)
video_encoding.reading: Representation-reconstruction video models (V-JEPA) beat contrastive CLIP; pixel-reconstruction (VideoMAE) lags. The objective matters more than the modality.

behavior_roar.reading: Chance floor 0.50; random-feature floor 0.54. GPT-2 from strings alone matches the human ceiling (0.811) — lexical decision is orthographic knowledge. Qwen via generation leads.

embodied_game.reading: Visual VLMs struggle with abstract-grid perception (3B below the random floor; 7B's solves are optimal-efficiency). A thinking model with perfect ASCII perception isolates perception as the bottleneck. The closed loop ran ~300 process(EnvironmentStep) calls with zero schema errors.

multimodal_algonauts.reading: Banded ridge over (video CLIP + audio Wav2Vec2 + text MiniLM) beats every single modality, replicating the Algonauts paper's multimodal baseline range (~0.20-0.25). MIRAGE's 0.319 uses a 30B gated encoder — the gap is backbone, not pipeline.

nulls.description: Every capability is validated against a matched null that runs through the same pipeline with the signal destroyed in one specific way.

entries.what_it_catches: alignment coincidence — score must degrade when mis-timed
non-specific damage — a curated population must beat a random one
the selected population is special only if it beats random

ablation.capability: Induced dyslexia via process(StateChange) — Qwen2.5-VL-3B on ROAR
ablation.reading: Ablating the pseudo-selective MLP population (K=500/layer) drops accuracy from 1.00 to 0.67 — across the 0.65 dyslexia threshold. A random same-size ablation barely moves it (0.91). reset() restores bit-for-bit.

selection.capability: Composite selection — units across layers for one region
selection.reading: A CompositeSelector gathers a functional population spanning depth into one region; the readout draws most from the middle-late layers.

inputs.desc: Still images drive the ventral stream; predicted V4/IT responses map onto occipitotemporal cortex.
Sentences drive the language network; a causal LM's features predict left frontotemporal language regions.
Short videos drive dorsal + ventral visual cortex; native-temporal V-JEPA leads.
Continuous movies drive much of cortex; banded ridge over video+audio+text beats any single stream.
